title: PressHeart: A Two-Factor Authentication Mechanism via PPG Signals for Wearable Devices
venue: 2024 IEEE International Conference on Communications (ICC): Communication and Information System Security Symposium

method.input_representation: Feature matrices consisting of 6 specific features (Systolic Amplitude, Ratio of Amplitude of Peak and Valley, Press Width, Ratio of Width of Crest and Trough, Number of Crests, Number of Troughs) and 15 general features extracted via tsfresh

evaluation_protocol: Leave-one-subject-out approach where one participant is the legitimate user and the remaining participants act as attackers (random attacks). Data randomly shuffled for training and testing.

reported_limitations: Implementation limited to a prototype rather than commercial devices

key_contribution: Proposed PressHeart, a two-factor authentication mechanism utilizing PPG sensors to capture physiological blood flow and behavioral press patterns.
main_strength: Theoretical resistance to presentation attacks due to behavioral component
main_weakness: Lack of empirical spoofing attack tests
insights.notes_for_review: The authors claim resistance to presentation attacks in Table II, but the experimental section only describes 'random attacks' (imposter attempts), which doesn't qualify as a spoofing/presentation attack test per the guidelines.
